objective: Rewrite Sage from a tool-protocol-first LangGraph loop to a plain-text-first, editable-response-session UX with backend-only tool execution and end-to-end legacy cleanup.
scope_summary: Reworked the LangGraph runtime, prompt contract, and Discord transport so assistant text is now the primary user-facing channel, tool calls are backend-only coordination, provisional drafts are streamed into one editable response session, and no-tool assistant turns finalize the conversation without terminal delivery tools.
constraints: Treated the rewrite as a hard breaking change with no compatibility layer for sage_finish_turn or tool-delivered normal chat replies.
Kept the touched scope focused on runtime, prompt, Discord transport, tests, docs, and required workflow artifacts.

changes.summary: Rewrote the graph loop so assistant text plus tool calls becomes a provisional draft, no-tool assistant turns finalize the turn, approval/pause outcomes use response-session semantics, and terminal closeout-tool logic was removed.
Replaced the old closeout/tool-delivery state with response-session-first graph types, status enums, artifact delivery metadata, and plain-text-first completion/stop semantics.
Updated runtime results and streaming callbacks so callers receive response-session metadata and live draft updates instead of only one final reply blob.
Added editable response-session handling so the first draft sends one Discord reply, later assistant updates edit it in place, and final overflow is sent only at finalization.
Rewrote the universal prompt contract to teach the model a plain-text-first, tool-assisted behavior pattern and removed all sage_finish_turn and tool-only closeout instructions.
Updated the architecture docs to describe the plain-text-first loop, response-session delivery model, and new completion/stop semantics.
Replaced legacy protocol-closeout expectations with tests for provisional draft updates, plain-text finalization, and response-session-based outcomes.

validations.notes: Lint passed after the runtime, prompt, transport, and test rewrites.
Unit and integration suites passed after updating the plain-text-first behavior and transport expectations.
TypeScript build passed with the new response-session types and runtime wiring.
Tracked docs checks passed after updating the architecture and runbook guidance.
Artifact schemas and canonical workflow layout validated successfully.
Workflow audit passed after the plan/report updates.
Local workflow docs link gate passed.

evidence: The runtime now accepts mixed assistant text plus tool calls and finalizes on assistant text without tool calls.
Discord reply handling now edits one primary response message across draft revisions instead of routing normal answers through tools.
Repo docs and tests no longer describe sage_finish_turn or plain text as a protocol violation.
decisions: Made assistant plain text the canonical user-facing surface and restricted direct Discord-visible tool output to special artifacts only.
Implemented response-session updates in the Discord transport by consuming streamed graph state rather than introducing a separate persisted transport subsystem in the same pass.
risks: Response-session message continuity is adapter-local for the current execution; persisted cross-run rebinding to the exact original Discord message id is not yet part of this rewrite.
